name: The Index Core Ontology
description: Core ontology defining fundamental types and relationships for The Index protocol

terms.label: Supports
terms.definition: The evidence or claim provides strong positive support for the target claim

terms.label: Contradicts
terms.definition: The evidence or claim is in direct opposition to the target claim

terms.label: Weakly Supports
terms.definition: The evidence or claim provides limited or partial support for the target claim
examples: Preliminary results weakly support the hypothesis
Correlational data weakly supports causal claim

terms.label: Refines
terms.definition: The claim narrows or specifies the scope of the target claim without contradicting it
examples: Claim refines the conditions under which the effect occurs
New claim refines the original hypothesis with additional parameters

terms.label: Generalizes
terms.definition: The claim broadens the scope of the target claim to a wider context
examples: Theory generalizes findings to other domains

terms.label: Depends On
terms.definition: The claim is logically or empirically dependent on the target claim
examples: Derived theorem depends on fundamental axioms
Applied result depends on theoretical foundation

terms.label: Conflicts With
terms.definition: The claim is inconsistent with the target claim but not necessarily a direct contradiction

terms.label: Experiment
terms.definition: Controlled empirical investigation designed to test hypotheses

terms.label: Observation
terms.definition: Systematic recording of phenomena without experimental manipulation

terms.label: Dataset
terms.definition: Structured collection of data compiled for analysis

terms.label: Simulation
terms.definition: Computational modeling of systems or processes

terms.label: Theorem
terms.definition: Formally proven mathematical or logical statement

terms.label: Meta-Analysis
terms.definition: Statistical synthesis of results from multiple studies

terms.label: Proposed
terms.definition: Claim has been submitted but lacks sufficient evidence for evaluation

terms.label: Supported
terms.definition: Claim has strong evidential support with supporting weight significantly exceeding contradicting weight
examples: Well-established scientific fact with multiple lines of evidence

terms.label: Contested
terms.definition: Claim has comparable supporting and contradicting evidence, indicating uncertainty

terms.label: Refuted
terms.definition: Claim has strong contradicting evidence with contradicting weight significantly exceeding supporting weight

terms.label: Deprecated
terms.definition: Claim has been superseded, retracted, or removed from active consideration via governance
examples: Outdated theory replaced by better explanation
